title: Repurchase Agreement
description:
A repurchase agreement, or 'repo', is a short-term agreement to sell securities in order to buy them back at a slightly higher price. (Investopedia)

A repurchase agreement (repo) is a form of short-term borrowing for dealers in government securities. In the case of a repo, a dealer sells government securities to investors, usually on an overnight basis, and buys them back the following day at a slightly higher price. (Investopedia)

Sale of securities together with an agreement for the seller to buy back the securities at a later date.
A repo is equivalent to a spot sale combined with a forward contract. For the seller of the security it is a repo; for the buyer of the security it is a reverse repurchase agreement. (ISO20022)

Agreement between two parties to sell a financial instrument or set of financial instruments and repurchase such instruments at an agreed future date. (ISO20022)

A repurchase agreement (repo) is a short-term secured loan: one party sells securities to another and agrees to repurchase those securities later at a higher price.

RepurchaseAgreementType.description: Indicates whether the repo is a general collateral repo or specific repo. (ISO20022)
